palette_hint: Background #0f1117 near-black with body text #d4d8e2 cool gray, accent #5b8dee electric blue and #e8a838 amber for highlights and warnings.
type_style: Headings in system ui-sans-serif (SF Pro / Segoe UI) at 600-700 weight, body and all table cells in monospace (ui-monospace / Consolas) for numeric precision, tight letter-spacing, technical feel.
description: Nucleon Benchmarks is an independent GPU comparison site presenting dense performance data, pricing tiers, and hardware specifications for eight current-generation graphics cards across five vendor lines.
constraints: All comparison tables have sticky <thead> with background #1a1d27 and a 2px solid #5b8dee bottom border; zebra striping alternates #0f1117 and #161922 rows.
Numeric columns (VRAM, TFLOPS, watts, price) are right-aligned, rendered in ui-monospace at 13px, min-width 90px per cell; text columns left-aligned at 14px.
Max content width 1200px centered with 24px horizontal page padding; table wrapper allows horizontal scroll on overflow with a custom scrollbar styled to 6px height, thumb color #5b8dee.
Custom radio buttons and checkboxes on the Configure page use a 16px circle/square built from border + background only, accent color #5b8dee checked state, no box-shadow or gradients.

Form inputs on Configure page have border 1px solid #2e3245, background #161922, text #d4d8e2, padding 8px 12px, border-radius 4px; focus state changes border to #5b8dee using :focus pseudo-class only.

page_specs.overview: Landing page with a 120px hero band containing the Nucleon wordmark and a one-sentence methodology statement. Below it, a summary comparison table of all eight GPUs (RTX 4090, RTX 4080 Super, RX 7900 XTX, RX 7800 XT, Arc A770, RTX 4070 Ti, RX 7600, Arc A580) showing five key stats—VRAM (GB), Render Score, Compute TFLOPS, TDP (W), and Street Price (USD)—with amber (#e8a838) highlight on the top-scoring cell per column.
page_specs.performance: Three stacked full-width tables covering Gaming (1080p, 1440p, 4K avg FPS in 12 titles), Compute (FP32 TFLOPS, FP16 TFLOPS, INT8 TOPS), and Rasterization vs Ray-Tracing scores; each table has its own sticky header row and a color-coded rank column (1–8) using inline background chips in #5b8dee for rank 1 and plain text for ranks 2–8.
page_specs.pricing: A pricing history table showing each GPU's launch MSRP, current street price, lowest recorded price, and price-per-TFLOPS ratio across the eight cards, followed by a secondary table comparing retailer availability (Amazon, B&H, Newegg, Microcenter) with in-stock indicators shown as filled (#5b8dee) or empty (#2e3245) 10px squares styled via CSS only.
page_specs.specifications: A dense side-by-side specification sheet with 28 rows of hardware attributes (architecture node, shader count, TMUs, ROPs, memory bus width, bandwidth, PCIe slot, power connectors, display outputs, etc.) for all eight GPUs; rows use zebra striping and cells with differing values from the category leader are marked with amber (#e8a838) text color.
page_specs.configure: A filtered comparison tool page with a multi-column form (two columns, 24px gutter) letting the user select which GPUs to compare (eight CSS-only custom checkboxes), a budget ceiling via a styled text input, a use-case priority radio group (Gaming / Compute / Mixed), and a minimum VRAM radio group (8 GB / 12 GB / 16 GB / 24 GB); below the form a static results table pre-filtered to the most common selection (Gaming, 16 GB max budget $900) with all form validation states shown via :required and :invalid CSS pseudo-classes on the budget input.
